output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 4/4.
Batch 1: Executed batch 1 task T1 in `src/features/admin_chat/tools.py`. The `share_to_social` schema now documents direct-post mode, adds `media_urls` as an array of strings, threads an optional `guild_id` through `execute_share_to_social`, and passes `trusted_guild_id` from the dispatcher. Checkpointed results to `.megaplan/plans/add-a-direct-post-mode-to-20260412-2125/execution_batch_1.json`. Verification for this batch was syntax-level only: `python -m py_compile src/features/admin_chat/tools.py` passed.
Batch 2: Executed batch 2 task T2 in `src/features/admin_chat/tools.py`. Added a direct-post branch to `execute_share_to_social` for calls without `message_link` or `message_id`: it validates direct-mode inputs, downloads `media_urls` to temp files, builds a `SocialPublishRequest` with sentinel IDs and `user_details={'direct_post': True}`, disables announce/first-share/legacy/duplicate policies, preserves temp files for scheduled enqueue, and cleans them up after immediate publish or failed download. Verification passed with `python -m py_compile src/features/admin_chat/tools.py`, `python -m pytest tests/test_caller_paths.py -q`, and an inline direct-mode harness covering immediate publish, scheduled enqueue without cleanup, and retweet validation. Checkpoint written to `.megaplan/plans/add-a-direct-post-mode-to-20260412-2125/execution_batch_2.json`.
Batch 3: Executed batch 3 task T3 in `src/features/admin_chat/agent.py`. Updated the admin chat system prompt so `share_to_social` explicitly documents direct posting without a Discord message using `tweet_text` and optional `media_urls`, and calls out retweets via `action=retweet` plus `target_post`. I also aligned the detailed tool synopsis with the higher-level media-tools instruction so the prompt no longer gives conflicting guidance about requiring a Discord message reference. Verification passed with `python -m py_compile src/features/admin_chat/agent.py` and direct text checks in the updated prompt. Checkpoint written to `.megaplan/plans/add-a-direct-post-mode-to-20260412-2125/execution_batch_3.json`.
Batch 4: Executed batch 4 task T4. The full test suite passed on `python -m pytest tests/ -x -q` with 217 passing tests and no modifications required. I then wrote a throwaway verification script in `/tmp`, ran it successfully, and deleted it. That script traced the direct-post code path for the required scenarios: direct media publish via `publish_now`, scheduled direct enqueue without cleanup, text-only direct post, direct retweet request construction, missing `tweet_text` validation for media posts, and message-link precedence where `media_urls` is ignored. Checkpoint written to `.megaplan/plans/add-a-direct-post-mode-to-20260412-2125/execution_batch_4.json`.
commands_run: rg -n "media_urls|direct posting with media_urls|guild_id: Optional\[int\] = None|guild_id=trusted_guild_id" src/features/admin_chat/tools.py

python - <<'PY'  # inline direct-mode verification harness
...
PY

rg -n "media_urls = params|getattr\(sharer, 'social_publish_service'|message_id=0|user_details': \{'direct_post': True\}|duplicate_policy=\{'check_existing': False\}|announce_policy=\{'enabled': False\}|first_share_notification_policy=\{'enabled': False\}|legacy_shared_post_policy=\{'enabled': False\}|preserve_downloads = True|guild_id is required for direct social posts" src/features/admin_chat/tools.py
rg -n "media_urls|direct posting without a Discord message|action=retweet and target_post|already_shared|publication_id" src/features/admin_chat/agent.py

task_updates.executor_notes: Updated `share_to_social` schema and plumbing in `src/features/admin_chat/tools.py`. Verified the description now documents direct-post mode at line 190, `media_urls` is defined as `type=array` with `items.type=string` at lines 206-211, `execute_share_to_social` accepts `guild_id: Optional[int] = None` at line 1810, and the dispatcher passes `guild_id=trusted_guild_id` at line 3969. Syntax verification passed with `python -m py_compile src/features/admin_chat/tools.py`.

Implemented the direct-post branch in `src/features/admin_chat/tools.py`. Verified `media_urls = params.get('media_urls') or []` is extracted at line 1816. In the no-message branch, direct mode validates retweet restrictions, requires `guild_id` for direct posts, downloads each URL through `sharer._download_media_from_url`, constructs `SocialPublishRequest` with sentinel IDs at lines 1938-1941, sets `source_context.metadata['user_details']` to `{'direct_post': True}` at line 1958, and disables duplicate detection plus announce/first-share/legacy policies at lines 1950-1954. Scheduled direct posts preserve temp files via `preserve_downloads = True` at line 1968; immediate publishes clean up in `finally`. Verification passed with `python -m py_compile src/features/admin_chat/tools.py`, `python -m pytest tests/test_caller_paths.py -q`, and an inline direct-mode harness covering immediate publish, scheduled enqueue without cleanup, and retweet validation.

python - <<'PY'  # inline direct-mode verification harness

PY

task_updates.executor_notes: Updated `src/features/admin_chat/agent.py` so the high-level `share_to_social` instruction at line 141 now mentions direct posting without a Discord message via `tweet_text` and optional `media_urls`, plus direct retweets via `action=retweet` and `target_post`. Also aligned the detailed tool synopsis at line 76 to the same behavior so the prompt is not self-contradictory. Verification passed with `python -m py_compile src/features/admin_chat/agent.py`, and text checks confirmed both prompt entries include `tweet_text`, `media_urls`, `publication_id`, `already_shared`, and direct retweet guidance.

Full-suite verification passed with `python -m pytest tests/ -x -q`: 217 tests passed, 4 warnings, no test modifications needed. A throwaway script was created in `/tmp`, run successfully, and deleted afterward. The script manually traced the required direct-post scenarios: `media_urls` + `tweet_text` downloads into a direct request and reaches `publish_now`; scheduled direct media reaches `enqueue` and preserves temp files; `tweet_text` alone produces a text-only direct request and reaches `publish_now`; `action=retweet` + `target_post` produces a retweet request with no text/media; missing `tweet_text` with `media_urls` returns a clear error; and `media_urls` alongside `message_link` stays in the message-based flow with `media_urls` ignored.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed in `src/features/admin_chat/tools.py` that `media_urls` uses `type=array` with `items.type=string`, and the `share_to_social` description explicitly documents direct-post mode with `media_urls + tweet_text`.

Confirmed in `src/features/admin_chat/tools.py` that the direct-post branch builds `SocialPublishRequest` with `source_context.metadata['user_details'] = {'direct_post': True}` and disables duplicate detection plus announce/first-share/legacy policies. The inline harness also verified the request shape and cleanup behavior.

Confirmed in `src/features/admin_chat/agent.py` that the updated prompt mentions direct posting with `tweet_text` and optional `media_urls`, and explicitly documents retweets via `action=retweet` plus `target_post`.

Confirmed the full suite passed without modification (`217 passed`), and the throwaway direct-post script traced all required scenarios: direct media publish, scheduled enqueue, text-only direct post, direct retweet request, missing `tweet_text` validation, and message-link precedence with `media_urls` ignored.
